NKJV.1: Now about that time Herod the king stretched out his hand to harass some from the church.
NKJV.2: Then he killed James the brother of John with the sword.
NKJV.3: And because he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded further to seize Peter also. Now it was during the Days of Unleavened Bread.
NKJV.4: So when he had arrested him, he put him in prison, and delivered him to four squads of soldiers to keep him, intending to bring him before the people after Passover.
NKJV.5: Peter was therefore kept in prison, but constant prayer was offered to God for him by the church.
NKJV.6: And when Herod was about to bring him out, that night Peter was sleeping, bound with two chains between two soldiers; and the guards before the door were keeping the prison.
NKJV.7: Now behold, an angel of the Lord stood by him, and a light shone in the prison; and he struck Peter on the side and raised him up, saying, “Arise quickly!” And his chains fell off his hands.
NKJV.8: Then the angel said to him, “Gird yourself and tie on your sandals”; and so he did. And he said to him, “Put on your garment and follow me.”
NKJV.9: So he went out and followed him, and did not know that what was done by the angel was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.
NKJV.10: When they were past the first and the second guard posts, they came to the iron gate that leads to the city, which opened to them of its own accord; and they went out and went down one street, and immediately the angel departed from him.
NKJV.11: And when Peter had come to himself, he said, “Now I know for certain that the Lord has sent His angel, and has delivered me from the hand of Herod and from all the expectation of the Jewish people.”
NKJV.12: So, when he had considered this, he came to the house of Mary, the mother of John whose surname was Mark, where many were gathered together praying.
NKJV.13: And as Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a girl named Rhoda came to answer.
NKJV.14: When she recognized Peter’s voice, because of her gladness she did not open the gate, but ran in and announced that Peter stood before the gate.
NKJV.15: But they said to her, “You are beside yourself!” Yet she kept insisting that it was so. So they said, “It is his angel.”
NKJV.16: Now Peter continued knocking; and when they opened the door and saw him, they were astonished.
NKJV.17: But motioning to them with his hand to keep silent, he declared to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he said, “Go, tell these things to James and to the brethren.” And he departed and went to another place.
NKJV.18: Then, as soon as it was day, there was no small stir among the soldiers about what had become of Peter.
NKJV.19: But when Herod had searched for him and not found him, he examined the guards and commanded that they should be put to death. And he went down from Judea to Caesarea, and stayed there.
NKJV.20: Now Herod had been very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon; but they came to him with one accord, and having made Blastus the king’s personal aide their friend, they asked for peace, because their country was supplied with food by the king’s country.
NKJV.21: So on a set day Herod, arrayed in royal apparel, sat on his throne and gave an oration to them.
NKJV.22: And the people kept shouting, “The voice of a god and not of a man!”
NKJV.23: Then immediately an angel of the Lord struck him, because he did not give glory to God. And he was eaten by worms and died.
NKJV.24: But the word of God grew and multiplied.
NKJV.25: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem when they had fulfilled their ministry, and they also took with them John whose surname was Mark.

ESV.1: About that time Herod the king laid violent hands on some who belonged to the church.
ESV.2: He killed James the brother of John with the sword,
ESV.3: and when he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also. This was during the days of Unleavened Bread.
ESV.4: And when he had seized him, he put him in prison, delivering him over to four squads of soldiers to guard him, intending after the Passover to bring him out to the people.
ESV.5: So Peter was kept in prison, but earnest prayer for him was made to God by the church.
ESV.6: Now when Herod was about to bring him out, on that very night, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and sentries before the door were guarding the prison.
ESV.7: And behold, an angel of the Lord stood next to him, and a light shone in the cell. He struck Peter on the side and woke him, saying, “Get up quickly.” And the chains fell off his hands.
ESV.8: And the angel said to him, “Dress yourself and put on your sandals.” And he did so. And he said to him, “Wrap your cloak around you and follow me.”
ESV.9: And he went out and followed him. He did not know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.
ESV.10: When they had passed the first and the second guard, they came to the iron gate leading into the city. It opened for them of its own accord, and they went out and went along one street, and immediately the angel left him.
ESV.11: When Peter came to himself, he said, “Now I am sure that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from the hand of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting.”
ESV.12: When he realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John whose other name was Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.
ESV.13: And when he knocked at the door of the gateway, a servant girl named Rhoda came to answer.
ESV.14: Recognizing Peter's voice, in her joy she did not open the gate but ran in and reported that Peter was standing at the gate.
ESV.15: They said to her, “You are out of your mind.” But she kept insisting that it was so, and they kept saying, “It is his angel!”
ESV.16: But Peter continued knocking, and when they opened, they saw him and were amazed.
ESV.17: But motioning to them with his hand to be silent, he described to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he said, “Tell these things to James and to the brothers.”  Then he departed and went to another place.
ESV.18: Now when day came, there was no little disturbance among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
ESV.19: And after Herod searched for him and did not find him, he examined the sentries and ordered that they should be put to death. Then he went down from Judea to Caesarea and spent time there.
ESV.20: Now Herod was angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon, and they came to him with one accord, and having persuaded Blastus, the king's chamberlain,  they asked for peace, because their country depended on the king's country for food.
ESV.21: On an appointed day Herod put on his royal robes, took his seat upon the throne, and delivered an oration to them.
ESV.22: And the people were shouting, “The voice of a god, and not of a man!”
ESV.23: Immediately an angel of the Lord struck him down, because he did not give God the glory, and he was eaten by worms and breathed his last.
ESV.24: But the word of God increased and multiplied.
ESV.25: And Barnabas and Saul returned from  Jerusalem when they had completed their service, bringing with them John, whose other name was Mark.

NASB.1: Now about that time Herod the king laid hands on some who belonged to the church in order to mistreat them.
NASB.2: And he had James the brother of John put to death with a sword.
NASB.3: When he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also. Now it was during the days of Unleavened Bread.
NASB.4: When he had seized him, he put him in prison, delivering him to four squads of soldiers to guard him, intending after the Passover to bring him out before the people.
NASB.5: So Peter was kept in the prison, but prayer for him was being made fervently by the church to God.
NASB.6: On the very night when Herod was about to bring him forward, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and guards in front of the door were watching over the prison.
NASB.7: And behold, an angel of the Lord suddenly appeared and a light shone in the cell; and he struck Peter's side and woke him up, saying, 'Get up quickly.' And his chains fell off his hands.
NASB.8: And the angel said to him, 'Gird yourself and put on your sandals.' And he did so. And he said to him, 'Wrap your cloak around you and follow me.'
NASB.9: And he went out and continued to follow, and he did not know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.
NASB.10: When they had passed the first and second guard, they came to the iron gate that leads into the city, which opened for them by itself; and they went out and went along one street, and immediately the angel departed from him.
NASB.11: When Peter came to himself, he said, 'Now I know for sure that the Lord has sent forth His angel and rescued me from the hand of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting.'
NASB.12: And when he realized [this], he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John who was also called Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.
NASB.13: When he knocked at the door of the gate, a servant-girl named Rhoda came to answer.
NASB.14: When she recognized Peter's voice, because of her joy she did not open the gate, but ran in and announced that Peter was standing in front of the gate.
NASB.15: They said to her, 'You are out of your mind!' But she kept insisting that it was so. They kept saying, 'It is his angel.'
NASB.16: But Peter continued knocking; and when they had opened [the door], they saw him and were amazed.
NASB.17: But motioning to them with his hand to be silent, he described to them how the Lord had led him out of the prison. And he said, 'Report these things to James and the brethren.' Then he left and went to another place.
NASB.18: Now when day came, there was no small disturbance among the soldiers [as to] what could have become of Peter.
NASB.19: When Herod had searched for him and had not found him, he examined the guards and ordered that they be led away [to execution]. Then he went down from Judea to Caesarea and was spending time there.
NASB.20: Now he was very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon; and with one accord they came to him, and having won over Blastus the king's chamberlain, they were asking for peace, because their country was fed by the king's country.
NASB.21: On an appointed day Herod, having put on his royal apparel, took his seat on the rostrum and [began] delivering an address to them.
NASB.22: The people kept crying out, 'The voice of a god and not of a man!'
NASB.23: And immediately an angel of the Lord struck him because he did not give God the glory, and he was eaten by worms and died.
NASB.24: But the word of the Lord continued to grow and to be multiplied.
NASB.25: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem when they had fulfilled their mission, taking along with [them] John, who was also called Mark.

NLT.1: About that time King Herod Agrippa began to persecute some believers in the church.
NLT.2: He had the apostle James (John’s brother) killed with a sword.
NLT.3: When Herod saw how much this pleased the Jewish people, he also arrested Peter. (This took place during the Passover celebration. )
NLT.4: Then he imprisoned him, placing him under the guard of four squads of four soldiers each. Herod intended to bring Peter out for public trial after the Passover.
NLT.5: But while Peter was in prison, the church prayed very earnestly for him.
NLT.6: The night before Peter was to be placed on trial, he was asleep, fastened with two chains between two soldiers. Others stood guard at the prison gate.
NLT.7: Suddenly, there was a bright light in the cell, and an angel of the Lord stood before Peter. The angel struck him on the side to awaken him and said, “Quick! Get up!” And the chains fell off his wrists.
NLT.8: Then the angel told him, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” And he did. “Now put on your coat and follow me,” the angel ordered.
NLT.9: So Peter left the cell, following the angel. But all the time he thought it was a vision. He didn’t realize it was actually happening.
NLT.10: They passed the first and second guard posts and came to the iron gate leading to the city, and this opened for them all by itself. So they passed through and started walking down the street, and then the angel suddenly left him.
NLT.11: Peter finally came to his senses. “It’s really true!” he said. “The Lord has sent his angel and saved me from Herod and from what the Jewish leaders had planned to do to me!”
NLT.12: When he realized this, he went to the home of Mary, the mother of John Mark, where many were gathered for prayer.
NLT.13: He knocked at the door in the gate, and a servant girl named Rhoda came to open it.
NLT.14: When she recognized Peter’s voice, she was so overjoyed that, instead of opening the door, she ran back inside and told everyone, “Peter is standing at the door!”
NLT.15: “You’re out of your mind!” they said. When she insisted, they decided, “It must be his angel.”
NLT.16: Meanwhile, Peter continued knocking. When they finally opened the door and saw him, they were amazed.
NLT.17: He motioned for them to quiet down and told them how the Lord had led him out of prison. “Tell James and the other brothers what happened,” he said. And then he went to another place.
NLT.18: At dawn there was a great commotion among the soldiers about what had happened to Peter.
NLT.19: Herod Agrippa ordered a thorough search for him. When he couldn’t be found, Herod interrogated the guards and sentenced them to death. Afterward Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while.
NLT.20: Now Herod was very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon. So they sent a delegation to make peace with him because their cities were dependent upon Herod’s country for food. The delegates won the support of Blastus, Herod’s personal assistant,
NLT.21: and an appointment with Herod was granted. When the day arrived, Herod put on his royal robes, sat on his throne, and made a speech to them.
NLT.22: The people gave him a great ovation, shouting, “It’s the voice of a god, not of a man!”
NLT.23: Instantly, an angel of the Lord struck Herod with a sickness, because he accepted the people’s worship instead of giving the glory to God. So he was consumed with worms and died.
NLT.24: Meanwhile, the word of God continued to spread, and there were many new believers.
NLT.25: When Barnabas and Saul had finished their mission to Jerusalem, they returned, taking John Mark with them.

WEB.1: Now about that time, King Herod stretched out his hands to oppress some of the assembly.
WEB.2: He killed James, the brother of John, with the sword.
WEB.3: When he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to seize Peter also. This was during the days of unleavened bread.
WEB.4: When he had arrested him, he put him in prison, and delivered him to four squads of four soldiers each to guard him, intending to bring him out to the people after the Passover.
WEB.5: Peter therefore was kept in the prison, but constant prayer was made by the assembly to God for him.
WEB.6: The same night when Herod was about to bring him out, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains. Guards in front of the door kept the prison.
WEB.7: And behold, an angel of the Lord stood by him, and a light shone in the cell. He struck Peter on the side, and woke him up, saying, “Stand up quickly!” His chains fell off from his hands.
WEB.8: The angel said to him, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” He did so. He said to him, “Put on your cloak, and follow me.”
WEB.9: And he went out and followed him. He didn’t know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he saw a vision.
WEB.10: When they were past the first and the second guard, they came to the iron gate that leads into the city, which opened to them by itself. They went out, and went down one street, and immediately the angel departed from him.
WEB.11: When Peter had come to himself, he said, “Now I truly know that the Lord has sent out his angel and delivered me out of the hand of Herod, and from everything the Jewish people were expecting.”
WEB.12: Thinking about that, he came to the house of Mary, the mother of John who was called Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.
WEB.13: When Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a maid named Rhoda came to answer.
WEB.14: When she recognized Peter’s voice, she didn’t open the gate for joy, but ran in, and reported that Peter was standing in front of the gate.
WEB.15: They said to her, “You are crazy!” But she insisted that it was so. They said, “It is his angel.”
WEB.16: But Peter continued knocking. When they had opened, they saw him, and were amazed.
WEB.17: But he, beckoning to them with his hand to be silent, declared to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. He said, “Tell these things to James, and to the brothers.” Then he departed, and went to another place.
WEB.18: Now as soon as it was day, there was no small stir among the soldiers about what had become of Peter.
WEB.19: When Herod had sought for him, and didn’t find him, he examined the guards, and commanded that they should be put to death. He went down from Judea to Caesarea, and stayed there.
WEB.20: Now Herod was very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon. They came with one accord to him, and, having made Blastus, the king’s personal aide, their friend, they asked for peace, because their country depended on the king’s country for food.
WEB.21: On an appointed day, Herod dressed himself in royal clothing, sat on the throne, and gave a speech to them.
WEB.22: The people shouted, “The voice of a god, and not of a man!”
WEB.23: Immediately an angel of the Lord struck him, because he didn’t give God the glory, and he was eaten by worms and died.
WEB.24: But the word of God grew and multiplied.
WEB.25: Barnabas and Saul returned to  Jerusalem, when they had fulfilled their service, also taking with them John who was called Mark.

CSB17.1: About that time King Herod violently attacked some who belonged to the church,
CSB17.2: and he executed James, <sup>ⓗ</sup> John’s brother, with the sword.
CSB17.3: When he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter too, during the Festival of Unleavened Bread. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.4: After the arrest, he put him in prison and assigned four squads of four soldiers each to guard him, intending to bring him out to the people after the Passover.
CSB17.5: So Peter was kept in prison, but the church was praying fervently to God for him.
CSB17.6: When Herod was about to bring him out for trial, that very night Peter, bound with two chains, was sleeping between two soldiers, while the sentries in front of the door guarded the prison.
CSB17.7: Suddenly an angel of the Lord <sup>ⓙ</sup> appeared, and a light shone in the cell. Striking Peter on the side, he woke him up and said, “Quick, get up!” And the chains fell off his wrists. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.8: “Get dressed,” the angel told him, “and put on your sandals.” And he did. “Wrap your cloak around you,” he told him, “and follow me.”
CSB17.9: So he went out and followed, and he did not know that what the angel did was really happening, but he thought he was seeing a vision. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.10: After they passed the first and second guards, they came to the iron gate that leads into the city, which opened to them by itself. They went outside and passed one street, and suddenly the angel left him. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.11: When Peter came to himself, he said, “Now I know for certain that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from Herod’s grasp and from all that the Jewish people expected.” <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.12: As soon as he realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John Mark, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓐ</sup> where many had assembled and were praying.
CSB17.13: He knocked at the door of the outer gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
CSB17.14: She recognized Peter’s voice, and because of her joy, she did not open the gate but ran in and announced that Peter was standing at the outer gate.
CSB17.15: “You’re out of your mind!” they told her. But she kept insisting that it was true, and they said, “It’s his angel.” <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.16: Peter, however, kept on knocking, and when they opened the door and saw him, they were amazed.
CSB17.17: Motioning to them with his hand <sup>ⓒ</sup> to be silent, he described to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. “Tell these things to James <sup>ⓓ</sup> and the brothers,” he said, and he left and went to another place.
CSB17.18: At daylight, there was a great commotion among the soldiers as to what had become of Peter.
CSB17.19: After Herod had searched and did not find him, he interrogated the guards and ordered their execution. Then Herod went down from Judea to Caesarea and stayed there. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.20: Herod had been very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon. Together they presented themselves before him. After winning over Blastus, who was in charge of the king’s bedroom, they asked for peace, because their country was supplied with food from the king’s country. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.21: On an appointed day, dressed in royal robes and seated on the throne, Herod delivered a speech to them.
CSB17.22: The assembled people began to shout, “It’s the voice of a god and not of a man!”
CSB17.23: At once an angel of the Lord <sup>ⓖ</sup> struck him because he did not give the glory to God, and he was eaten by worms and died. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.24: But the word of God flourished and multiplied. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.25: After they had completed their relief mission, Barnabas and Saul returned to <sup>[2]</sup> Jerusalem, taking along John who was called Mark. <sup>ⓙ</sup>

AMP.1: Now at that time Herod [Agrippa I] the king [of the Jews] arrested some who belonged to the church, intending to harm them.
AMP.2: And he had James the brother of John put to death with a sword;
AMP.3: and when he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to have Peter arrested as well. This was during the days of Unleavened Bread [the Passover week].
AMP.4: When he had seized Peter, he put him in prison, turning him over to four squads of soldiers of four each to guard him [in rotation throughout the night], planning after the Passover to bring him out before the people [for execution].
AMP.5: So Peter was kept in prison, but fervent and persistent prayer for him was being made to God by the church.
AMP.6: The very night before Herod was to bring him forward, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and sentries were in front of the door guarding the prison.
AMP.7: Suddenly, an angel of the Lord appeared [beside him] and a light shone in the cell. The angel struck Peter's side and awakened him, saying, "Get up quickly!" And the chains fell off his hands.
AMP.8: The angel said to him, "Prepare yourself and strap on your sandals [to get ready for whatever may happen]." And he did so. Then the angel told him, "Put on your robe and follow me."
AMP.9: And Peter went out following the angel. He did not realize that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.
AMP.10: When they had passed the first guard and the second, they came to the iron gate that leads into the city. Of its own accord it swung open for them; and they went out and went along one street, and at once the angel left him.
AMP.11: When Peter came to his senses, he said, "Now I know for certain that the Lord has sent His angel and has rescued me from the hand of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting [to do to me]."
AMP.12: When he realized what had happened, he went to the house of Mary the mother of John, who was also called Mark, where many [believers] were gathered together and were praying continually [and had been praying all night].
AMP.13: When he knocked at the door of the gateway, a servant-girl named Rhoda came to answer.
AMP.14: Recognizing Peter's voice, in her joy she failed to open the gate, but ran in and announced that Peter was standing in front of the gateway.
AMP.15: They said to her, "You are out of your mind!" But she kept insisting that it was so. They kept saying, "It is his angel!"
AMP.16: But [meanwhile] Peter continued knocking; and when they opened the door and saw him, they were completely amazed.
AMP.17: But motioning to them with his hand to be quiet and listen, he described how the Lord had led him out of the prison. And he said, "Report these things to James and the brothers and sisters." Then he left and went to another place.
AMP.18: Now when day came, there was no small disturbance among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
AMP.19: When Herod had searched for him and could not find him, he interrogated the guards and commanded that they be led away to execution. Then he went down from Judea to Caesarea [Maritima] and spent some time there.
AMP.20: Now Herod [Agrippa I] was extremely angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon; and their delegates came to him in a united group, and after persuading Blastus, the king's chamberlain [to support their cause], they asked for peace, because their country was fed by [imports of grain and other goods from] the king's country.
AMP.21: On an appointed day Herod dressed himself in his royal robes, sat on his throne (tribunal, rostrum) and began delivering a speech to the people.
AMP.22: The assembled people kept shouting, "It is the voice of a god and not of a man!"
AMP.23: And at once an angel of the Lord struck him down because he did not give God the glory [and instead permitted himself to be worshiped], and he was eaten by worms and died [five days later].
AMP.24: But the word of the Lord [the good news about salvation through Christ] continued to grow and spread [increasing in effectiveness].
AMP.25: Barnabas and Saul came back from Jerusalem when they had completed their mission, bringing with them John, who was also called Mark.

MSG.1: That's when King Herod got it into his head to go after some of the church members.
MSG.3: When he saw how much it raised his popularity ratings with the Jews, he arrested Peter—all this during Passover Week, mind you—
MSG.4: and had him thrown in jail, putting four squads of four soldiers each to guard him. He was planning a public lynching after Passover.
MSG.5: All the time that Peter was under heavy guard in the jailhouse, the church prayed for him most strenuously.
MSG.6: Then the time came for Herod to bring him out for the kill. That night, even though shackled to two soldiers, one on either side, Peter slept like a baby. And there were guards at the door keeping their eyes on the place. Herod was taking no chances!
MSG.7: Suddenly there was an angel at his side and light flooding the room. The angel shook Peter and got him up: "Hurry!" The handcuffs fell off his wrists.
MSG.8: The angel said, "Get dressed. Put on your shoes." Peter did it. Then, "Grab your coat and let's get out of here."
MSG.9: Peter followed him, but didn't believe it was really an angel—he thought he was dreaming.
MSG.10: Past the first guard and then the second, they came to the iron gate that led into the city. It swung open before them on its own, and they were out on the street, free as the breeze. At the first intersection the angel left him, going his own way.
MSG.11: That's when Peter realized it was no dream. "I can't believe it—this really happened! The Master sent his angel and rescued me from Herod's vicious little production and the spectacle the Jewish mob was looking forward to."
MSG.12: Still shaking his head, amazed, he went to Mary's house, the Mary who was John Mark's mother. The house was packed with praying friends.
MSG.13: When he knocked on the door to the courtyard, a young woman named Rhoda came to see who it was.
MSG.14: But when she recognized his voice—Peter's voice!—she was so excited and eager to tell everyone Peter was there that she forgot to open the door and left him standing in the street.
MSG.15: But they wouldn't believe her, dismissing her, dismissing her report. "You're crazy," they said. She stuck by her story, insisting. They still wouldn't believe her and said, "It must be his angel."
MSG.16: All this time poor Peter was standing out in the street, knocking away. Finally they opened up and saw him—and went wild!
MSG.17: Peter put his hands up and calmed them down. He described how the Master had gotten him out of jail, then said, "Tell James and the brothers what's happened." He left them and went off to another place.
MSG.18: At daybreak the jail was in an uproar. "Where is Peter? What's happened to Peter?"
MSG.19: When Herod sent for him and they could neither produce him nor explain why not, he ordered their execution: "Off with their heads!" Fed up with Judea and Jews, he went for a vacation to Caesarea.
MSG.20: But things went from bad to worse for Herod. Now people from Tyre and Sidon put him on the warpath. But they got Blastus, King Herod's right-hand man, to put in a good word for them and got a delegation together to iron things out. Because they were dependent on Judea for food supplies, they couldn't afford to let this go on too long.
MSG.21: On the day set for their meeting, Herod, robed in pomposity, took his place on the throne and regaled them with a lot of hot air.
MSG.22: The people played their part to the hilt and shouted flatteries: "The voice of God! The voice of God!"
MSG.23: That was the last straw. God had had enough of Herod's arrogance and sent an angel to strike him down. Herod had given God no credit for anything. Down he went. Rotten to the core, a maggoty old man if there ever was one, he died.
MSG.24: Meanwhile, the ministry of God's Word grew by leaps and bounds.
MSG.25: Barnabas and Saul, once they had delivered the relief offering to the church in Jerusalem, went back to Antioch. This time they took John with them, the one they called Mark.

NIV.1:
Peter’s Miraculous Escape From Prison
It was about this time that King Herod arrested some who belonged to the church, intending to persecute them.
NIV.2: He had James, the brother of John, put to death with the sword.
NIV.3: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he proceeded to seize Peter also. This happened during the Feast of Unleavened Bread.
NIV.4: After arresting him, he put him in prison, handing him over to be guarded by four squads of four soldiers each. Herod intended to bring him out for public trial after the Passover.
NIV.5: So Peter was kept in prison, but the church was earnestly praying to God for him.
NIV.6: The night before Herod was to bring him to trial, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and sentries stood guard at the entrance.
NIV.7: Suddenly an angel of the Lord appeared and a light shone in the cell. He struck Peter on the side and woke him up. “Quick, get up!” he said, and the chains fell off Peter’s wrists.
NIV.8: Then the angel said to him, “Put on your clothes and sandals.” And Peter did so. “Wrap your cloak around you and follow me,” the angel told him.
NIV.9: Peter followed him out of the prison, but he had no idea that what the angel was doing was really happening; he thought he was seeing a vision.
NIV.10: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city. It opened for them by itself, and they went through it. When they had walked the length of one street, suddenly the angel left him.
NIV.11: Then Peter came to himself and said, “Now I know without a doubt that the Lord sent his angel and rescued me from Herod’s clutches and from everything the Jewish people were anticipating.”
NIV.12: When this had dawned on him, he went to the house of Mary the mother of John, also called Mark, where many people had gathered and were praying.
NIV.13: Peter knocked at the outer entrance, and a servant girl named Rhoda came to answer the door.
NIV.14: When she recognized Peter’s voice, she was so overjoyed she ran back without opening it and exclaimed, “Peter is at the door!”
NIV.15: “You’re out of your mind,” they told her. When she kept insisting that it was so, they said, “It must be his angel.”
NIV.16: But Peter kept on knocking, and when they opened the door and saw him, they were astonished.
NIV.17: Peter motioned with his hand for them to be quiet and described how the Lord had brought him out of prison. “Tell James and the brothers about this,” he said, and then he left for another place.
NIV.18: In the morning, there was no small commotion among the soldiers as to what had become of Peter.
NIV.19:
After Herod had a thorough search made for him and did not find him, he cross-examined the guards and ordered that they be executed.
Herod’s Death
Then Herod went from Judea to Caesarea and stayed there a while.
NIV.20: He had been quarreling with the people of Tyre and Sidon; they now joined together and sought an audience with him. Having secured the support of Blastus, a trusted personal servant of the king, they asked for peace, because they depended on the king’s country for their food supply.
NIV.21: On the appointed day Herod, wearing his royal robes, sat on his throne and delivered a public address to the people.
NIV.22: They shouted, “This is the voice of a god, not of a man.”
NIV.23: Immediately, because Herod did not give praise to God, an angel of the Lord struck him down, and he was eaten by worms and died.
NIV.24: But the word of God continued to increase and spread.
NIV.25: When Barnabas and Saul had finished their mission, they returned from Jerusalem, taking with them John, also called Mark.

NRSVCE.1: About that time King Herod laid violent hands upon some who belonged to the church.
NRSVCE.2: He had James, the brother of John, killed with the sword.
NRSVCE.3: After he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also. (This was during the festival of Unleavened Bread.)
NRSVCE.4: When he had seized him, he put him in prison and handed him over to four squads of soldiers to guard him, intending to bring him out to the people after the Passover.
NRSVCE.5: While Peter was kept in prison, the church prayed fervently to God for him.
NRSVCE.6: The very night before Herod was going to bring him out, Peter, bound with two chains, was sleeping between two soldiers, while guards in front of the door were keeping watch over the prison.
NRSVCE.7: Suddenly an angel of the Lord appeared and a light shone in the cell. He tapped Peter on the side and woke him, saying, “Get up quickly.” And the chains fell off his wrists.
NRSVCE.8: The angel said to him, “Fasten your belt and put on your sandals.” He did so. Then he said to him, “Wrap your cloak around you and follow me.”
NRSVCE.9: Peter went out and followed him; he did not realize that what was happening with the angel’s help was real; he thought he was seeing a vision.
NRSVCE.10: After they had passed the first and the second guard, they came before the iron gate leading into the city. It opened for them of its own accord, and they went outside and walked along a lane, when suddenly the angel left him.
NRSVCE.11: Then Peter came to himself and said, “Now I am sure that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from the hands of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting.”
NRSVCE.12: As soon as he realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John whose other name was Mark, where many had gathered and were praying.
NRSVCE.13: When he knocked at the outer gate, a maid named Rhoda came to answer.
NRSVCE.14: On recognizing Peter’s voice, she was so overjoyed that, instead of opening the gate, she ran in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
NRSVCE.15: They said to her, “You are out of your mind!” But she insisted that it was so. They said, “It is his angel.”
NRSVCE.16: Meanwhile Peter continued knocking; and when they opened the gate, they saw him and were amazed.
NRSVCE.17: He motioned to them with his hand to be silent, and described for them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he added, “Tell this to James and to the believers.” Then he left and went to another place.
NRSVCE.18: When morning came, there was no small commotion among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
NRSVCE.19: When Herod had searched for him and could not find him, he examined the guards and ordered them to be put to death. Then he went down from Judea to Caesarea and stayed there.
NRSVCE.20: Now Herod was angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon. So they came to him in a body; and after winning over Blastus, the king’s chamberlain, they asked for a reconciliation, because their country depended on the king’s country for food.
NRSVCE.21: On an appointed day Herod put on his royal robes, took his seat on the platform, and delivered a public address to them.
NRSVCE.22: The people kept shouting, “The voice of a god, and not of a mortal!”
NRSVCE.23: And immediately, because he had not given the glory to God, an angel of the Lord struck him down, and he was eaten by worms and died.
NRSVCE.24: But the word of God continued to advance and gain adherents.
NRSVCE.25: Then after completing their mission Barnabas and Saul returned to Jerusalem and brought with them John, whose other name was Mark.

NET.1: About that time King Herod laid hands on some from the church to harm them.
NET.2: He had James, the brother of John, executed with a sword.
NET.3: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter too. (This took place during the feast of Unleavened Bread.)
NET.4: When he had seized him, he put him in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to bring him out for public trial after the Passover.
NET.5: So Peter was kept in prison, but those in the church were earnestly praying to God for him.
NET.6: On that very night before Herod was going to bring him out for trial, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, while guards in front of the door were keeping watch over the prison.
NET.7: Suddenly an angel of the Lord appeared, and a light shone in the prison cell. He struck Peter on the side and woke him up, saying, “Get up quickly!” And the chains fell off Peter’s wrists.
NET.8: The angel said to him, “Fasten your belt and put on your sandals.” Peter did so. Then the angel said to him, “Put on your cloak and follow me.”
NET.9: Peter went out and followed him; he did not realize that what was happening through the angel was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.
NET.10: After they had passed the first and second guards, they came to the iron gate leading into the city. It opened for them by itself, and they went outside and walked down one narrow street, when at once the angel left him.
NET.11: When Peter came to himself, he said, “Now I know for certain that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from the hand of Herod and from everything the Jewish people were expecting to happen.”
NET.12: When Peter realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John Mark, where many people had gathered together and were praying.
NET.13: When he knocked at the door of the outer gate, a slave girl named Rhoda answered.
NET.14: When she recognized Peter’s voice, she was so overjoyed she did not open the gate, but ran back in and told them that Peter was standing at the gate.
NET.15: But they said to her, “You’ve lost your mind!” But she kept insisting that it was Peter, and they kept saying, “It is his angel!”
NET.16: Now Peter continued knocking, and when they opened the door and saw him, they were greatly astonished.
NET.17: He motioned to them with his hand to be quiet and then related how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. He said, “Tell James and the brothers these things,” and then he left and went to another place.
NET.18: At daybreak there was great consternation among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
NET.19: When Herod had searched for him and did not find him, he questioned the guards and commanded that they be led away to execution. Then Herod went down from Judea to Caesarea and stayed there.
NET.20: Now Herod was having an angry quarrel with the people of Tyre and Sidon. So they joined together and presented themselves before him. And after convincing Blastus, the king’s personal assistant, to help them, they asked for peace, because their country’s food supply was provided by the king’s country.
NET.21: On a day determined in advance, Herod put on his royal robes, sat down on the judgment seat, and made a speech to them.
NET.22: But the crowd began to shout, “The voice of a god, and not of a man!”
NET.23: Immediately an angel of the Lord struck Herod down because he did not give the glory to God, and he was eaten by worms and died.
NET.24: But the word of God kept on increasing and multiplying.
NET.25: So Barnabas and Saul returned to Jerusalem when they had completed their mission, bringing along with them John Mark.
